title: Epitaph On Dr. Johnson.
content:
آ آ آ آ Here Johnson liesï؟½a sage by all allowï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ Whom to have bred may well make England proud,
آ آ آ آ Whose prose was eloquence, by wisdom taught,
آ آ آ آ The graceful vehicle of virtuous thought;
آ آ آ آ Whose verse may claimï؟½grave, masculine, and strongï؟½
آ آ آ آ Superior praise to the mere poetï؟½s song;
آ آ آ آ Who many a noble gift from heaven possessï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And faith at last, alone worth all the rest.
آ آ آ آ O man, immortal by a double prize,
آ آ آ آ By fame on earthï؟½by glory in the skies!
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epitaph On Dr. Johnson."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
